contraindications.clinicalRationale: RAS inhibitors may need dose adjustment or alternative therapy in advanced CKD with eGFR <25 ml/min per 1.73 m² as evidenced by recommendation for alternative therapy (nonsteroidal MRA) at this threshold
contraindications.riskDescription: Reduced efficacy and potential for progression of kidney disease without alternative nephroprotective therapy
contraindications.alternativeConsiderations: Nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with proven kidney or cardiovascular benefit
contraindications.sourceSnippet: We suggest a nonsteroidal mineralocorticoid receptor antagonist with proven kidney or cardiovascular benefit for patients with T2D, an eGFR >=25 ml/min per 1.73 m2, normal serum potassium concentration, and albuminuria (>=30 mg/g [>=3 mg/mmol]) despite maximum tolerated dose of RAS inhibitor (2A).
